mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and income expansion, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 139,435 thousand suggests ongoing but moderating employment growth
regime_hint: post-crisis expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth below 0.2%, the zero-lower-bound regime persists, signaling extended monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 237.428 indicate continued low-rate, low-inflation environment
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary regime with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns in triggered periods are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or if Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 900 thousand units signals weakening housing demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900 thousand units in month-over-month comparison
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 956.0 thousand is near but above the trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market normalization
failure_condition: Negative HOUST signals fail to produce negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.1% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 5.8%, signaling simultaneous supply weakness and labor market softening that triggers equity repricing downward over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.1152 and UNRATE at 5.9% are near trigger levels
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration with emerging labor market slack
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails if 20-day forward returns in triggered periods are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.3% or t-test p > 0.10), or if Rank IC > -0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month, indicating strong consumer demand and forward earnings momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporations benefit from sustained spending.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 442,689 million dollars reflects ongoing consumer activity
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS acceleration fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and forward earnings risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.9% provides baseline for measuring deterioration
regime_hint: labor market inflection point or early recession signal
failure_condition: Unemployment deterioration fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus 3-month trailing CPIAUCSL growth) turn positive and exceed 0.5% while GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2% annualized, signaling monetary tightening in a growing economy that compresses equity multiples and produces negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: (FEDFUNDS - 3mo trailing CPIAUCSL growth) > 0.5% AND GDPC1 annualized growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and GDPC1 at 16010.4 billion suggest low real rates in growing economy
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle in expansion phase
failure_condition: Real rate tightening signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 950 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) show month-over-month growth above 0.3%, jointly signaling broad-based demand strength that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings expansion and multiple expansion.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 950 thousand AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 956.0 and RSAFS at 442,689 million are near trigger thresholds
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Joint demand signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
